meta.title: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. | Software Product Development, AI Delivery & Digital Systems
meta.description: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. (XICO AI) delivers software products, AI applications, web and mobile systems, and digital platforms from requirement definition to stable launch.

hero.subtitle: XICO is a software technology company. We build our own products and help companies and founders move from requirement definition and interaction design to engineering, launch, and ongoing operations. The goal is straightforward: ship systems that can run, scale, and keep improving.
hero.stack: Software Product Development · AI Application Delivery · Web Platforms · Mobile Apps · Business Systems

capabilities.title: From requirements to launch, delivery has to be solid
capabilities.subtitle: We look beyond feature lists: product scope, user flows, technical architecture, data boundaries, and the maintenance cost after launch.

items.title: AI Application Delivery
items.desc: We integrate model capability into real workflows: knowledge bases, AI Q&A, content processing, retrieval, evaluation, permissions, and data boundaries.

items.title: Web and Mobile Products
items.desc: From customer-facing products to admin operations, we cover information architecture, interaction flows, design systems, full-stack engineering, and responsive delivery.

items.title: Business Systems and Platforms
items.desc: Orders, memberships, content, payments, data, and operation consoles designed around workflows, permissions, and management efficiency.

items.title: Long-term Technical Foundation
items.desc: Clear architecture, maintainable code, deployment, monitoring, security boundaries, and performance work that reduce rework after launch.

work.title: Our own products keep our delivery accountable
work.subtitle: Machi, Shangence, and our study-abroad and cross-border directions are long-term product lines under Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. They test our methods in real business conditions.

items.name: Machi
items.tagline: A service entry point for life abroad
items.desc: Local information, services, and community connection for students, new arrivals, and people learning a city.
items.about: Machi is built for people living abroad: students, new immigrants, and anyone still learning local rules and available resources. City information, community, housing, jobs, second-hand listings, local services, and lived experience are brought into one entry point so users can move faster from search to action.

items.name: Shangence
items.tagline: Decision support before opening a business
items.desc: Risk assessment, validation budget, and action planning for small founders.
items.about: Shangence helps founders make a more grounded decision before the first major spend. It reviews industry conditions, capital safety, personal capability, acquisition difficulty, and external constraints, then returns a risk score, validation budget, stop-loss line, and 30-day action plan. It does not promise success; it gives the decision better evidence.

items.name: Businesses in incubation
items.tagline: Extending product capability into defined industry scenarios
items.desc: We are developing product lines for study abroad, cross-border commerce, and local services across China, Japan, and overseas markets.
items.about: Study abroad, cross-border commerce, and local services are the next fields XICO will continue to explore. We bring software engineering, experience design, and operational tooling into specific industries so products can serve real transactions and long-term operations.

approach.title: Good software starts with clear scope and ownership
approach.subtitle: Product judgment, experience design, and engineering have to move together.

principles.title: Define the problem and boundaries
principles.desc: Requirements are not a feature list. They are tradeoffs across users, cost, timing, data, and business scope.

principles.title: Turn workflows into clear interfaces
principles.desc: Interfaces are not decoration. Hierarchy, feedback, permissions, and edge states determine operating efficiency.

principles.title: Write code that can be maintained
principles.desc: Clear structure, module boundaries, and data models make a project easier to iterate, hand over, and extend.

principles.title: Design for multiple markets early
principles.desc: Chinese, Japanese, English, compliance, payments, and user habits should enter the plan before launch.

cta.title: Before building a system, align the goal and the scope.
cta.subtitle: Bring us a new product, a legacy system, or a business preparing to launch. We will help define scope, priority, technical route, and delivery cadence.

footer.tagline: Software products, AI applications, and digital platforms. We make complex work clear, and useful systems last.

about.title: A software technology company built around product delivery.
about.subtitle: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. operates self-built products, software development, and AI application delivery under XICO AI｜智希可.
story: We started in Changsha and work toward China, Japan, and wider overseas markets. We build our own products, and we open our product design, software development, and launch operations capability to external projects.

We care about technology, but we care more about whether it enters a real workflow. A product works only when the requirement is clear, the interface is usable, the system is stable, and the team can maintain it after launch.

Our working method is direct: confirm the business goal and user flow, then define scope, technical route, and iteration cadence. Each stage should be explainable, reviewable, and ready for the next step.

items.title: Validate value before building
items.desc: Not every requirement belongs in the first version. Confirm the goal, users, cost, and validation method first.

items.title: Experience decides whether a system gets used
items.desc: Interfaces need to be clear, stable, and predictable so users know what to do next.

items.title: Engineering must support the next version
items.desc: After launch come maintenance, expansion, fixes, and growth. The codebase needs room for later versions.

founder.name: Yao Kai · 姚凱
founder.quote: A product is not a page. It is a system that can be used and maintained over time.
bio: I started XICO to put product judgment, experience design, and engineering execution into one delivery system. Many projects fail not because there is too little code, but because the problem, boundary, and priority were never made clear.

I tend to look at products from a full-stack point of view: front end, mobile, back end, database, payments, admin, deployment, and operations all have to connect. If one part is weak, users simply experience the system as unreliable.

Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. will keep building its own products while opening its software development and product delivery capability to partners. We want to become a technology company that can deliver complex projects reliably.

process.title: From first conversation to stable launch
process.subtitle: The process is not complicated, but each step has to be clean.

steps.title: Diagnosis and tradeoffs
steps.desc: We clarify goals, users, budget, timing, and the core functions that must ship.

steps.title: Prototype and plan
steps.desc: We map workflows, screens, and the technical route so risks appear early.

steps.title: Build and integrate
steps.desc: Front end, back end, database, permissions, payments, and deployment move forward by version, with clear acceptance points.

steps.title: Launch and iterate
steps.desc: After launch, we read data and feedback, fix what needs fixing, and leave the product in a sustainable operating state.

faq.title: A few things to clarify before working together
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, just write and ask.

items.q: Are you more of a software development company or a product studio?
items.a: Both. XICO builds long-term products of its own, and also opens its product, design, development, and launch capability to external collaborations.

items.q: Can we talk if the idea is still rough?
items.a: Yes. Many projects start incomplete. We will first help judge whether the need is real, how small the first version should be, and which features can wait.

items.q: What kinds of projects do you usually take on?
items.a: Software products, AI applications, web and mobile products, admin systems, digital platforms, company websites, and brand experience.

items.q: How long does a project usually take?
items.a: It depends on scope. A usable first version usually takes weeks to a few months. We prefer to make the first version clear and solid, then iterate.

items.q: Do you support the product after launch?
items.a: Yes. Deployment, monitoring, fixes, feature iteration, content, and data maintenance can continue as the project needs.
Send an email or fill out the contact form. Tell us what you want to build, where the project stands, and the expected timing. We usually reply within 1-2 business days.

manifesto.title: Clear, reliable, maintainable.
body: We evaluate a project by more than whether the interface is finished. The workflow has to run, data has to be reliable, permissions have to be clear, and deployment and maintenance have to be sustainable.
Software is not a one-time deliverable. A valuable system can launch, be used, be fixed, and keep expanding into the next stage.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. (“XICO AI”, “we”) respects and protects your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect on xicoai.com, how we use it, and the rights you have.

sections.p: When you contact us through a form, a partnership enquiry or a job application, we collect the information you choose to provide — such as your name, email, company, message, and the résumé file you upload when applying. We also collect basic technical logs (time of visit, pages, device type) to keep the site secure and stable.

We use this information only to respond to your enquiries, process collaboration or job applications, and operate and improve the site. We do not sell your personal information or use it for unrelated purposes.
Information is stored on servers under our control and protected with reasonable technical and organizational measures. We keep it only as long as needed for the purposes above.
We use only essential local storage to remember your theme (light/dark) and language preference. We do not use it for cross-site tracking.
You can contact us at hi@xicoai.com at any time to access, correct or delete your personal information.
We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on this page. Questions? Email hi@xicoai.com.

Unless otherwise stated, the text, design, marks and content of this site belong to XICO AI and may not be copied or used commercially without permission. Third-party brand marks belong to their respective owners.
Content on this site is provided “as is”. We aim to keep it accurate but make no warranty as to its completeness or timeliness.
To the extent permitted by law, XICO AI is not liable for any indirect or incidental loss arising from use of this site.
These terms are governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China.
For any questions about these terms, contact hi@xicoai.com.
